title: Mitra Dynamic Landings manifest
description: Spec for the content-linked dynamic-landing building block. PRODUCED by content-factory (the marketing engine), CONSUMED by mitra-website's generate_dynamic_pages.py. One entry = one ad-matched landing page at /<lang>/dynamic/<slug>. The ad's creative goes in the hero; the ad's web text becomes the headline/subtext. See content-factory/docs/spec-dynamic-landings.md.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601; when content-factory last produced this file.

slug.description: URL segment. Page builds to /<lang>/dynamic/<slug>. Must be unique per lang.

status.description: Only 'active' entries are generated into pages.

source.description: Provenance — which content/ad this landing matches. For attribution + traceability.

platform.description: facebook | instagram | linkedin | x | tiktok | google_ads | null

campaign.description: content-factory burst id, or paid campaign name.

ad_id.description: Paid-ad id when this is a paid placement; null for organic.

image.description: URL of the ad creative (the exact graphic the user saw). null renders the empty template placeholder.

aspect.description: Aspect of the creative frame; match the actual creative so it isn't letterboxed.

headline_html.description:
The ad's web text headline. Inline HTML allowed (,
). Single concept, matches the ad.

href.description: {{app_base}} is resolved per environment by build.py. UTM params from 'utm' are appended at generate time.

utm.description: Stamped onto the CTA href so the click attributes to this ad. Keep consistent with the ad's link.
